possible_causes: NTC plug not plugged in or has come loose
multiple plug on the PCB not plugged in correctly

source_refs.source_quote: F.00 Fault: Flow temperature sensor NTC plug not plugged in or has come loose, multiple plug on the PCB not plugged in correctly, interruption in cable harness, NTC sensor defective

possible_causes: NTC plug not plugged in or has come loose
multiple plug on the PCB not plugged in correctly

source_refs.source_quote: F.01 Fault: Return temperature sensor NTC plug not plugged in or has come loose, multiple plug on the PCB not plugged in correctly, interruption in cable harness, NTC sensor defective

possible_causes: short circuit in the cable harness

source_refs.source_quote: F.10 Short circuit: Flow temperature sensor NTC sensor defective, short circuit in the cable harness, cable/casing

possible_causes: short circuit in the cable harness

source_refs.source_quote: F.11 Short circuit: Return temperature sensor NTC sensor defective, short circuit in the cable harness, cable/casing

possible_causes: short circuit in the cable harness

source_refs.source_quote: F.12 and F.91 Short circuit: Cylinder temperature sensor NTC sensor defective, short circuit in the cable harness, cable/casing

fault_codes.description: Short circuit: Domestic hot water cylinder temperature sensor
possible_causes: short circuit in the cable harness

source_refs.source_quote: F.13 Short circuit: Domestic hot water cylinder temperature sensor NTC sensor defective, short circuit in the cable harness, cable/casing

possible_causes: Incorrect earth connection between cable harness and product
flow or return NTC defective (loose connection)

source_refs.source_quote: F.20 Safety switch-off: Overheating temperat- ure reached Incorrect earth connection between cable harness and product, flow or return NTC defective (loose connection), black discharge via ignition cable, ignition plug or ignition electrode

fault_codes.description: Safety switch-off: Temperature difference too great (NTC1/NTC2)
possible_causes: flow and return NTC sensors connected the wrong way round

source_refs.source_quote: F.23 Safety switch-off: Temperature difference too great (NTC1/NTC2) Pump blocked, insufficient pump output, air in product, flow and return NTC sensors connected the wrong way round

fault_codes.description: Safety switch-off: Temperature rise too fast

source_refs.source_quote: F.24 Safety switch-off: Temperature rise too fast Pump blocked, insufficient pump output, air in product, system pressure too low, non-return valve blocked/incorrectly installed

fault_codes.description: Safety switch-off: Flue gas temperature too high
possible_causes: Break in plug connection for optional flue gas safety cut-out (STB)

source_refs.source_quote: F.25 Safety switch-off: Flue gas temperature too high Break in plug connection for optional flue gas safety cut-out (STB), break in cable harness

fault_codes.description: Safety switch-off: Fault in flame detection

source_refs.source_quote: F.27 Safety switch-off: Fault in flame detection Moisture on the electronics, electronics (flame monitor) defective, gas solenoid valve leaking

fault_codes.description: Fault: Ignition unsuccessful when starting up
possible_causes: Gas meter defective or gas pressure monitor has triggered
multiple plug on PCB incorrectly plugged in
ignition system (ignition transformer, ignition cable, ignition plug, ignition electrode) defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.28 Fault: Ignition unsuccessful when starting up Gas meter defective or gas pressure monitor has triggered, air in gas, gas flow pressure too low, thermal isolator device (TAE) has triggered, incorrect gas restrictor, incorrect spare gas valve, fault on the gas valve, multiple plug on PCB incorrectly plugged in, break in cable harness, ignition system (ig- nition transformer, ignition cable, ignition plug, ignition electrode) defective, ionisation current interrupted (cable, electrode), incorrect earthing of product, electronics defective
F.29 Fault: Flame loss Gas supply temporarily stopped, flue gas recirculation, incorrect earthing of product, ignition transformer has spark failure

fault_codes.description: Fan frost protection function active: Fan speed outside the tolerance values
possible_causes: Plug on fan not correctly plugged in
multiple plug on PCB not correctly plugged in

source_refs.source_quote: F.32 Fan frost protection function active: Fan speed outside the tolerance values Plug on fan not correctly plugged in, multiple plug on PCB not correctly plugged in, break in cable harness, fan blocked, Hall sensor defective, elec- tronics defective

possible_causes: eBUS overload or two power supplies with different polarities on the eBUS

source_refs.source_quote: F.49 eBUS fault: Voltage too low Short circuit on eBUS, eBUS overload or two power supplies with different polarities on the eBUS

possible_causes: Short circuit/short to earth in cable harness for the gas valve
gas valve defective (coils shorted to earth)

source_refs.source_quote: F.61 Fault: Gas valve control system Short circuit/short to earth in cable harness for the gas valve, gas valve defective (coils shorted to earth), electronics defective
F.62 Fault: Gas valve switch-off control Delayed switch-off of gas valve, delayed extinguishing of flame signal, gas valve leaking, electronics defective
F.63 Fault: EEPROM Electronics defective

possible_causes: Flow or return NTC short circuited

source_refs.source_quote: F.64 Fault: Electronics/sensor/analogue-to- digital converter Flow or return NTC short circuited, electronics defective

possible_causes: Electronics overheating due to external influences

source_refs.source_quote: F.65 Fault: Electronics temperature too high Electronics overheating due to external influences, electronics defective

fault_codes.description: Value sent back by ASIC is incorrect (flame signal)

source_refs.source_quote: F.67 Fault: Value sent back by ASIC is incorrect (flame signal) Implausible flame signal, electronics defective
F.68 Fault: Unstable flame (analogue input) Air in gas, gas flow pressure too low, incorrect air ratio, incorrect gas re- strictor, ionisation flow interruption (cable, electrode)

possible_causes: Display and PCB replaced at same time and Device Specific Number not reset
wrong or missing output range coding resistance

source_refs.source_quote: F.70 Invalid product code (DSN) Display and PCB replaced at same time and Device Specific Number not reset, wrong or missing output range coding resistance

possible_causes: Flow temperature sensor signalling constant value
Flow temperature sensor incorrectly positioned on supply pipe

source_refs.source_quote: F.71 Fault: Flow/return temperature sensor Flow temperature sensor signalling constant value: Flow temperature sensor incorrectly positioned on supply pipe, flow temperature sensor defective

fault_codes.description: Fault: Deviation in the water pressure sensor/return temperature sensor
possible_causes: Flow/return NTC temperature difference too great
flow and/or return temperature sensor defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.72 Fault: Deviation in the water pressure sensor/return temperature sensor Flow/return NTC temperature difference too great → flow and/or return tem-perature sensor defective
F.77 Fault: Condensate or smoke No response, flue non-return flap defective

fault_codes.description: Interruption to DHW outlet sensor at external controller
possible_causes: UK link box is connected, but hot water NTC not bridged

source_refs.source_quote: F.78 Interruption to DHW outlet sensor at external controller UK link box is connected, but hot water NTC not bridged

possible_causes: When the burner starts, the temperature change registered at the flow or return temperature sensor is non-existent or too small
the flow or return temperature sensor is not in the correct position on the pipe

source_refs.source_quote: F.83 Fault: Dry fire When the burner starts, the temperature change registered at the flow or return temperature sensor is non-existent or too small: Insufficient water in the product, the flow or return temperature sensor is not in the correct position on the pipe

possible_causes: Flow and return temperature sensors signalling implausible values
Flow and return temperature sensors have been inverted
flow and return temperature sensors have not been correctly installed

source_refs.source_quote: F.84 Fault: Flow/return temperature sensor Values not consistent, difference < -6 K Flow and return temperature sensors signalling implausible values: Flow and return temperature sensors have been inverted, flow and return temperature sensors have not been correctly installed

possible_causes: The flow and/or return temperature sensors have been installed on the same pipe/incorrect pipe
Temperature sensor not connected or is connected incorrectly

source_refs.source_quote: F.85 Fault: Temperature sensor The flow and/or return temperature sensors have been installed on the same pipe/incorrect pipe Temperature sensor not connected or is connected incorrectly
F.86 Fault: Underfloor heating contact Underfloor heating contact open, sensor disconnected or defective

possible_causes: Electrodes not connected or they are connected incorrectly
short circuit in the cable harness

source_refs.source_quote: F.87 Fault: Electrodes Electrodes not connected or they are connected incorrectly, short circuit in the cable harness

possible_causes: Gas valve not connected or it is connected incorrectly
short circuit in the cable harness

source_refs.source_quote: F.88 Fault: Gas valve Gas valve not connected or it is connected incorrectly, short circuit in the cable harness

fault_codes.description: No communication between the PCB and the user interface

source_refs.source_quote: Connection No communication between the PCB and the user interface Electronics defective
d.00 Heating maximum output kW The maximum heating output varies depending on the product. → Section "Technical data" Adjustable
d.01 Pump overrun in heating mode 1 60 min 1 5 Adjustable

diagnostic_codes.description: Burner anti-cycling time in heating mode

source_refs.source_quote: d.02 Burner anti-cycling time 2 60 min 1 20 Adjustable in heating mode
d.04 Water temperature in the Current value °C Not cylinder adjustable

diagnostic_codes.description: Determined heating flow set target temperature

source_refs.source_quote: d.05 Determined heating flow Current value °C Not set target temperature adjustable

diagnostic_codes.description: Set target temperature for the domestic hot water cylinder

source_refs.source_quote: d.07 Set target temperature Current value °C Not for the domestic hot water adjustable cylinder
d.08 Status of the 230 V ther- Current value 0 = Room thermostat open (no heat Not mostat requirement) adjustable 1 = Room thermostat closed (heat requirement)

diagnostic_codes.description: Heating flow set target temperature that is set on the eBUS room thermostat

source_refs.source_quote: d.09 Heating flow set target Current value Not temperature that is set on the adjustable eBUS room thermostat

diagnostic_codes.description: Status of the internal pump in the heating circuit

source_refs.source_quote: d.10 Status of the internal Current value off / on Not pump in the heating circuit adjustable

diagnostic_codes.description: Status of the heating circuit's shunt pump

source_refs.source_quote: d.11 Status of the heating cir- Current value off / on Not cuit's shunt pump adjustable

diagnostic_codes.description: Status of the hot water circuit's circulation pump

source_refs.source_quote: d.13 Status of the hot water Current value off / on Not circuit's circulation pump adjustable

diagnostic_codes.description: Status of the 24 V room thermostat

source_refs.source_quote: d.16 Status of the 24 V room Current value off = Heating off Not thermostat on = Heating on adjustable

d.17 Heating control off = Flow temperature 0 Adjustable on = Return temperature (adjustment for underfloor heating. If you have ac- tivated the return temperature control, the automatic heating output determ- ination function is not active.)

d.18 Pump overrun operating 1 3 1 Adjustable mode 1 = Comfort (continuously operating pump) 3 = Eco (intermittent pump mode – for the dissipation of the residual heat after hot water generation at an ex- tremely low heat demand)

diagnostic_codes.description: Maximum hot water set target temperature

source_refs.source_quote: d.20 Maximum hot water set 50 60 °C 1 50 Adjustable target temperature
d.23 Status of the heating de- Current value off = Heating off (Summer mode) Not mand on = Heating on adjustable
d.24 Status of the pressure 0 1 off = Not switched Not monitor on = Switched adjustable

diagnostic_codes.description: Status of the requirement to reheat the cylinder or for the hot water warm start from the eBUS thermostat

source_refs.source_quote: d.25 Status of the requirement Current value off = Function deactivated Not to reheat the cylinder or for on = Function activated adjustable the hot water warm start from the eBUS thermostat

d.27 Function of relay 1 (multi- 1 10 1 Adjustable functional module) 1 = Circulation pump 2 = External pump 3 = Cylinder charging pump 4 = Extractor hood 5 = External solenoid valve 6 = External fault message 7 = Solar pump (omitted) 8 = eBUS remote control 9 = Legionella protection pump 10 Solar valve

d.28 Function of relay 2 (multi- 1 10 2 Adjustable functional module) 1 = Circulation pump 2 = External pump 3 = Cylinder charging pump 4 = Extractor hood 5 = External solenoid valve 6 = External fault message 7 = Solar pump (omitted) 8 = eBUS remote control 9 = Legionella protection pump 10 = Solar valve
d.31 Automatic filling device 0 2 0 Adjustable 0 = Manual 1 = Semi-automatic 2 = Automatic
d.33 Fan speed target value Current value rpm Fan speed = Display value x 100 Not adjustable
d.34 Value for the fan speed Current value rpm Fan speed = Display value x 100 Not adjustable
d.40 Heating flow temperature Current value °C Not adjustable
d.41 Heating return temperat- Current value °C Not ure adjustable
